title: metate signal set
description: Cheap tier-1 capture of a bug/anomaly found mid-flow (currently smoke; the review write-side is not yet wired) without derailing. Appended to the profile's `signalsFile` (e.g. `.metate/signals.json`); NOT a tracker issue. `discover`/`triage` read this, confirm+rank, and stamp status: chosen signals become `promoted` (their candidate went into a plan; `prep` then files the issue), rejected ones `invalid`/`wontfix`. Every signal ends promoted or closed — nothing lives here forever.

title.description: One-sentence statement of the observed defect.

repro.description: Shortest steps seen so far to reproduce; 'unknown' is allowed at capture time.

evidence.description: Logs / command output / assertion diff / screenshot path backing the observation.

attribution.description: Ownership, decided by `git diff <base>`. in-diff = regression this sprint caused (a blocker, not a signal — fix in-branch). out-of-diff = pre-existing. exposed-latent = old bug only reachable because of this sprint's change.

severityGuess.description: Capture-time guess. S0 down/data-loss/security; S1 core broken no workaround; S2 workaround exists; S3 annoyance/cosmetic. Triage may revise.

blocksDoD.description: Does this block the current sprint's Definition of Done? out-of-diff + true is the only case that earns an interrupt decision (hotfix-first | scope-expand).

foundIn.description: Where it surfaced, e.g. 'smoke:T3'. Anchors it to the moment of capture.

status.description: Loop-closing state. open = awaiting triage; promoted = chosen into a plan by discover (prep files the issue next); invalid/wontfix = closed with reason. Nothing stays open forever.
